2606.14639.title: From Self-Supervised Speech Models to Mixture-of-Experts for Robust Anti-Spoofing
2606.14639.paper_summary: Recent advances in speech generation have significantly improved the naturalness of synthetic speech, making spoofing detection increasingly challenging. A key limitation of current anti-spoofing systems is their limited robustness to unseen synthesis methods. In this work, we transform a self-supervised speech representation model into a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture to improve generalization. Feed-forward blocks in selected encoder layers are replaced by multiple expert networks controlled by a layer-wise gating mechanism, allowing experts to capture complementary acoustic patterns while preserving the representations learned during self-supervised pretraining. We further analyze the architectural choices affecting the performance of this MoE conversion and investigate the activation behavior of the experts. The proposed approach is evaluated on 14 spoofing datasets and reduces the macro EER from 5.46% to 4.81%, corresponding to 11.9% relative improvement over the baseline.
2606.14639.paper_summary_zh: 近期在語音生成方面的進展顯著提高了合成語音的自然性，使得偽造檢測變得越來越具挑戰性。當前反偽造系統的一個主要限制是對未見合成方法的穩健性不足。在本研究中，我們將自我監督的語音表示模型轉換為混合專家（MoE）架構，以改善泛化能力。選定編碼器層中的前饋區塊被多個專家網絡所取代，這些專家網絡由逐層的閘控機制控制，使專家能夠捕捉互補的聲學模式，同時保留在自我監督預訓練期間學到的表示。我們進一步分析影響此MoE轉換性能的架構選擇，並研究專家的激活行為。所提出的方法在14個偽造數據集上進行評估，將宏觀EER從5.46%降低至4.81%，相當於相對於基準的11.9%的改善。
2606.14639.author: Hugo Daumain et.al.
2606.14639.authors: Hugo Daumain, Driss Matrouf, Khaled Khelif, Mickael Rouvier
